title: Fields3 — Collected Papers on Quantum Foundations and Biology (Chris Fields)
subtitle: Quantum entanglement, measurement, the classical-quantum boundary, and the nature of physical reality

summary.one_line: A collection of Chris Fields' papers on quantum information, the measurement problem, quantum biology, the classical-quantum boundary, and the implications of quantum mechanics for the nature of re...
summary.scope: Covers quantum measurement and decoherence, the role of the observer in quantum mechanics, quantum biology and entanglement in biological systems, the classical-quantum boundary, Fields' work with Levin and Glazebrook on quantum cognition and biological information processing.

sources.label: Chris Fields — Fields3: Collected Papers (C.N. Yang Institute for Theoretical Physics). PDF, 4.1 MB.

body.text: Fields' work addresses the measurement problem from an information-theoretic perspective. Quantum measurement is not a physical interaction but the establishment of a classical record — an irreversible information transfer that resolves quantum superpositions into definite outcomes. This maps directly to the constraint space formalism: measurement is S0 committing to a specific configuration within C. The observer does not cause collapse; the observer IS the system for which collapse is registered.

gaps: Full extraction of all collected papers needed
